title: Delta-scoped comment-citation gate remains unbuilt, recorded only inside closed FGAP-136
description: FGAP-136 (closed) proposed extending citation-rot-scanner.ts's detection surface to also cover code comments, delta-scoped so only a NEWLY-INTRODUCED comment citation in a diff is flagged (never forking a second parallel gate -- scan-comment-citations.ts already had to hand-duplicate part of the scanner's regex because CITATION_RE is unexported with no package subpath). FGAP-136's own closed_by states this gate remains unbuilt, filed as future work, not part of that closure. Closed items are not a work queue and are invisible to the hedge scanner and every other derive-state surface -- future work recorded only in a closed item's prose is effectively untracked.

evidence.reference: CITATION_RE is unexported with no package subpath; scanTsFile scopes operator-visible surfaces only, deliberately excluding JSDoc/line comments per its own test suite
TASK-107's enumeration tool exits 0 unconditionally -- informational only, not a gate
FGAP-136 closed_by verbatim: the proposed delta-scoped extension of citation-rot-scanner.ts to gate future comment citations remains unbuilt -- filed here as future work, not part of this closure

impact: No gate catches a newly-introduced comment-embedded tracker-ID citation going forward; the ~500+ pre-existing instances FGAP-136's rollout cleaned up could silently reaccumulate one new citation at a time with nothing flagging it, and this project's own PM model (which derives state from open items only) has no visibility into the fact this future work exists at all.
proposed_resolution: Export citation-rot-scanner.ts's CITATION_RE (or a small detection-core function) via a package subpath so scan-comment-citations.ts (and any future consumer) reuses it rather than hand-duplicating the regex. Extend the scanner's own detection surface to also scan JSDoc/line comments in source files, gated delta-scoped (only a citation introduced in the current diff/working-tree change is flagged, never a pre-existing one) via a git-diff-aware check, so the gate can go live today without retroactively blocking on the ~500+ already-cleaned instances.
